lesson: Dripping from the spout between serves points at the valve piston seal above the door — a failing O-ring weeps at the door seam instead. Where the mix shows up is what separates the two.
symptoms: Product dripping from the spout between serves.
Puddle forms on the drip tray within minutes.
A slow drip continues after the draw handle closes.
The leak comes directly from the serving spout.
Machine freezes and dispenses normally otherwise.
Every portion finishes with another portion's worth in the drip tray.
The door seam stays dry, but the spout keeps weeping.

tests.inspect-beater: Mix weeping around the dispense head — the seal faces are wet. The door casting itself sits true, no cracks.

flavour: Sealed the slow financial drain in the drip tray.
authenticityNote: Dispense valve piston seals wear with use and begin to weep between portions. Visually similar to a door O-ring leak but originating higher up inside the valve body — inspect-beater confirms it.
